blurb: Triangular grey-green leaves stacked and spiralling around the stem so it looks threaded through the middle, edges pinking up in sun. An easy, architectural spiller.
size: Stacked stems ~6–8 in, sprawling with age

care.light: Bright light with some direct sun for tight spacing and rosy edges; it stretches and the spiral opens up in shade.
care.water: Soak-and-dry, watering more in the cool growing season and easing off in summer heat.
care.dormancy: A winter/cool-season grower that rests through the hottest part of summer.
care.feeding: A dilute feed once or twice while growing.
care.potting: Repot as it lengthens; cut back leggy stems to keep it bushy.
care.propagation: Stem cuttings root very easily — lay a piece on dry mix.
care.bloom: Small pale-yellow star flowers cluster near the stem tips in spring.
care.troubles: Legginess in low light and root rot from overwatering; watch for mealybugs, aphids, and vine weevils.
care.toxicity: Like other Crassula, mildly toxic to cats and dogs if eaten; the sap can also irritate skin, so handle cuttings with a little care.
